mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in industrial production signals that recessionary output contraction is reversing, raising near-term earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends are followed by non-positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals renewed labor-market deterioration that lowers household income expectations and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: recession or early recovery with elevated labor slack
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances, or if mean triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5% and industrial production is rising over 3 months, monetary policy is highly accommodative while real activity improves, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS and INDPRO condition does not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than either condition alone, or if triggered average returns are not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in retail sales signals improving final demand and near-term revenue growth, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when consumers are leading the recovery.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS 3-month changes are followed by below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below their 12-month average signals renewed weakness in construction, credit creation, and household balance sheets, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change < 0 and HOUST below 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST weakness is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or if its return spread versus non-triggered periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment rises over 3 months while unemployment falls, labor income and confidence improve together, increasing expected sales and reducing recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-up and unemployment-down signal has no positive incremental return versus PAYEMS alone, or if triggered 20-day returns are negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month acceleration in CPI while the federal funds rate is not falling signals tighter real financial conditions and higher discount-rate risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation change > 0 and FEDFUNDS 3-month change >= 0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPI acceleration with non-easing policy is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth reading after prior weakness confirms a transition from contraction to expansion, raising expected earnings breadth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change crosses from <= 0 to > 0
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 positive-growth crossings are not followed by above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if walk-forward hit rate after crossings is below 50%.
